setupSQL: CREATE TABLE students (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);

INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (2, 'Monitor', 150.0);

INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department, salary) VALUES (1, 'Ada', 'Engineering', 75000);
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department, salary) VALUES (2, 'Lina', 'Design', 60000);

exampleQueries.sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders);
SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id = (SELECT customer_id FROM orders ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1);
SELECT name FROM customers WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders WHERE orders.customer_id = customers.id);

exampleQueries.sql: WITH high_earners AS (SELECT name FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000) SELECT * FROM high_earners;
WITH totals AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS t FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT customer_id, t FROM totals WHERE t > 100;

setupSQL: CREATE TABLE orders (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, total REAL);
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, total) VALUES (1, 1, 150);
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, total) VALUES (2, 2, 80);
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, total) VALUES (3, 3, 200);
challenges.starterSQL: WITH big_orders AS (SELECT customer_id, total FROM orders WHERE total > 100) SELECT customer_id, total FROM big_orders ORDER BY total DESC;

exampleQueries.sql: SELECT name, score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY score DESC) AS rank FROM students;
SELECT name, salary, AVG(salary) OVER () AS avg_salary FROM employees;
SELECT name, score, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY score DESC) AS rank FROM students;
